Possible Symptom in Plant: Pale green / light yellow older leaves; slow growth
Recommended Dose (kg/ha or as noted):
60–80 kg N/ha
(130–175 kg Urea/ha)
Application Method & Timing: Broadcast + incorporate before sowing OR split: 50% basal, 25% at tillering, 25% at panicle initiation
Caution / Side Effect: Avoid applying when soil is dry or waterlogged; volatilization loss if surface-applied without irrigation

Possible Symptom in Plant: Yellow leaves starting from base; stunted plants; early leaf drop
Recommended Dose (kg/ha or as noted):
100 kg DAP/ha
+ foliar spray: 2% Urea
Application Method & Timing: Basal application + foliar spray every 7 days for 3 applications
Caution / Side Effect: DAP raises pH slightly; retest pH after application; excess N causes lodging in cereals

Possible Symptom in Plant: Dark green leaves; excessive vegetative growth; delayed maturity
Primary Fertilizer / Amendment: Stop N application; apply more irrigation to leach
Recommended Dose (kg/ha or as noted): Increase irrigation by 20–30% to leach excess NO₃⁻
Application Method & Timing: Stop all N fertilizers; apply potassium to balance; allow natural depletion
Caution / Side Effect: Excess N increases pest/disease susceptibility; delays harvest

Possible Symptom in Plant: Purple/reddish tinge on undersides of leaves; slow root development
Recommended Dose (kg/ha or as noted):
375–500 kg SSP/ha
(60–80 kg P₂O₅/ha)
Application Method & Timing: Basal incorporation before planting; band placement near root zone preferred
Secondary / Alternate Option:
Rock Phosphate
(direct application for acid soils)
Caution / Side Effect: P fixation is high in red/laterite soils of Telangana; use band placement to reduce fixation

Possible Symptom in Plant: Stunted root growth; poor flowering; poor fruit/grain set; dark purple color
Recommended Dose (kg/ha or as noted):
100–150 kg DAP/ha
+ foliar: 2% DAP spray
Application Method & Timing: Basal + one foliar spray at vegetative stage; combine with mycorrhizal inoculant
Caution / Side Effect: P interacts with Zn — excess P causes Zn deficiency; monitor zinc levels

Possible Symptom in Plant: Similar to P deficiency even when P was applied
Application Method & Timing: Incorporate 4–6 weeks before sowing to allow acidification by soil bacteria
Caution / Side Effect: Slow acting; retest pH after 6–8 weeks; sulfur oxidation needs moisture and warm soil

Possible Symptom in Plant: Scorching / brown edges on older leaves; weak stems
Recommended Dose (kg/ha or as noted):
100–120 kg MOP/ha
(60–72 kg K₂O/ha)
Application Method & Timing: Basal or split: 50% at planting, 50% at knee-high stage; avoid direct seed contact
Caution / Side Effect: Avoid MOP for tobacco & potato (Cl-sensitive crops); use SOP instead

Possible Symptom in Plant: Marginal leaf scorch on older leaves; poor fruit quality; increased disease
Recommended Dose (kg/ha or as noted):
150–200 kg SOP/ha
+ foliar: 1% KNO₃
Application Method & Timing: Basal incorporation + emergency foliar spray with 1% potassium nitrate every 10 days for 3 doses
Caution / Side Effect: Sandy soils have low K retention; split applications essential in such soils

Possible Symptom in Plant: Calcium & magnesium deficiency induced by K antagonism
Recommended Dose (kg/ha or as noted): 400–500 kg dolomite/ha
Application Method & Timing: Broadcast and incorporate; rebalance Ca:Mg:K ratio
Caution / Side Effect: K:Mg ratio should be < 3:1; excess K also locks out boron

Possible Symptom in Plant: Multiple nutrient deficiencies; Al & Mn toxicity; poor microbial activity
Recommended Dose (kg/ha or as noted):
1000–2000 kg/ha
(based on soil buffer pH)
Application Method & Timing: Incorporate 4–6 weeks before planting; apply in dry weather; retest after 8 weeks
Secondary / Alternate Option:
Dolomitic Lime
CaMg(CO₃)₂ if Mg also low
Caution / Side Effect: Over-liming causes Mn and Zn deficiency; increase dose gradually; max 2 t/ha per season

Possible Symptom in Plant: Mild Al toxicity; reduced P availability; reduced legume nodulation
Application Method & Timing: Broadcast and incorporate before planting; apply with irrigation
Caution / Side Effect: Wood ash also provides K and Ca; test wood ash nutrient content before use

Possible Symptom in Plant: P, Fe, Mn, Zn, B, Cu deficiencies; sodium toxicity possible
Recommended Dose (kg/ha or as noted):
500–1000 kg S/ha
(to lower pH by 1 unit in loam)
Application Method & Timing: Incorporate 6–8 weeks before planting; maintain soil moisture for bacterial oxidation
Secondary / Alternate Option:
Gypsum (for sodic soils)
+ foliar Fe/Zn/Mn spray
Caution / Side Effect: Sulfur needs warm moist soil & Thiobacillus bacteria to work; acidified water for drip irrigation helps

Possible Symptom in Plant: Poor germination; leaf burn; wilting despite adequate moisture
Recommended Dose (kg/ha or as noted):
2000–5000 kg/ha
(based on SAR value)
Application Method & Timing: Broadcast and irrigate heavily to leach salts; use flood irrigation in first season
Secondary / Alternate Option:
Press mud / FYM
+ sulfuric acid drip
Caution / Side Effect: Requires good drainage to leach salts; use salt-tolerant crop varieties during reclamation

Possible Symptom in Plant: Wilting; leaf roll; poor nutrient uptake; blossom drop
Recommended Dose (kg/ha or as noted):
Restore to 40–60% VWC
Drip: 3–5 mm/day (vegetables)
Flood: 50–75 mm/week (rice)
Application Method & Timing: Apply in early morning or evening; split into 2–3 shorter sessions to improve infiltration
Caution / Side Effect: Combine with deficit irrigation for water-stressed regions; use tensiometer for precision

Possible Symptom in Plant: Root rot; yellowing; anaerobic odor; wilting despite water
Recommended Dose (kg/ha or as noted):
Create furrows / raised beds;
install subsurface drainage tiles at 60–90 cm depth
Application Method & Timing: Stop all irrigation; create drainage furrows immediately; apply Trichoderma for root rot
Caution / Side Effect: Waterlogging for > 48 hrs causes irreversible root damage in most vegetable crops

Possible Symptom in Plant: Heat stress; flower drop; poor pollination; sunscald
Recommended Dose (kg/ha or as noted):
Install shade net at 6–8 ft height;
use reflective mulch
Application Method & Timing: Increase irrigation frequency; use kaolin spray (5%) to reflect radiation
Caution / Side Effect: Kaolin clay spray is safe and effective for horticulture crops; reapply after rain

Possible Symptom in Plant: Chilling injury; frost damage; slow growth; color break in fruits
Recommended Dose (kg/ha or as noted):
Cover crops with 30–50 GSM agro fabric
at night; smoke pots for severe frost
Application Method & Timing: Apply potassium fertilizer to improve cold hardiness; avoid excess N before cold season
Secondary / Alternate Option:
Irrigation (flood) for frost
protection in orchards
Caution / Side Effect: High K improves cold tolerance by improving cell osmotic potential

Possible Symptom in Plant: Blossom drop; tip burn; spider mite infestation; poor fruit set
Recommended Dose (kg/ha or as noted):
Run misting 4–6 times/day
for 5–10 min each; target leaf wetness index < 8 hrs to avoid disease
Application Method & Timing: Mulching; windbreaks; increase plant density
Caution / Side Effect: High humidity misting increases fungal disease risk; balance with good air circulation

Possible Symptom in Plant: Fungal diseases (blight, powdery mildew, botrytis); root rot
Recommended Dose (kg/ha or as noted):
Create wider row spacing;
pruning to improve canopy ventilation
Application Method & Timing:
Copper-based fungicide spray (preventive);
Potassium silicate to strengthen cell walls
Secondary / Alternate Option:
Calcium nitrate foliar spray
(strengthens cell walls)
Caution / Side Effect: Potassium silicate (2–3 ml/L) significantly reduces fungal penetration

Possible Symptom in Plant: Little leaf; shortened internodes; mottled young leaves
Recommended Dose (kg/ha or as noted):
25 kg ZnSO₄/ha (soil)
or foliar: 0.5% ZnSO₄ + 0.25% lime
Application Method & Timing: Basal incorporation OR foliar spray at 2-leaf stage; repeat after 10 days
Caution / Side Effect: Most effective foliar spray in alkaline soils where soil Zn is fixed; acidify spray water

Possible Symptom in Plant: Cracked stems; hollow fruits; flower drop; distorted young leaves
Recommended Dose (kg/ha or as noted):
1–2 kg Borax/ha (soil)
or foliar: 0.1–0.2% Borax spray
Application Method & Timing: Mix in sand before broadcast; foliar spray at bud initiation; repeat in 10 days
Caution / Side Effect: NEVER exceed 2 kg B/ha soil — boron toxicity is very difficult to reverse

Condition / Deficiency Level:
Deficiency – Interveinal
chlorosis in young leaves
Possible Symptom in Plant: Young leaves yellow between green veins; alkaline soil common cause
Recommended Dose (kg/ha or as noted):
25–50 kg FeSO₄/ha (soil)
or foliar: 0.5% FeSO₄ + 0.1% citric acid
Application Method & Timing: Soil: mix with compost to prevent fixation; foliar: spray with acidified water at 7-day interval x3
Secondary / Alternate Option:
Chelated Iron (Fe-EDTA or
Fe-DTPA) for alkaline soils
Caution / Side Effect: Fe soil application is ineffective in pH > 7.0; use chelated DTPA form for such soils

Possible Symptom in Plant: Yellow between veins on older leaves; early leaf drop
Recommended Dose (kg/ha or as noted):
50–100 kg MgSO₄/ha (soil)
or foliar: 2% MgSO₄ spray
Application Method & Timing: Basal or top-dress; foliar spray at 15-day interval; 2–3 applications
Secondary / Alternate Option:
Dolomite Lime
(if soil is also acidic)
Caution / Side Effect: Common in sandy soils and high-K soils; K:Mg ratio should be < 3:1

Possible Symptom in Plant: Blossom end rot in tomato/pepper; tip burn in cabbage; cracked fruit
Recommended Dose (kg/ha or as noted):
Foliar: 0.5–1% CaNO₃ spray
every 7–10 days from fruit set
Application Method & Timing: Foliar spray at fruit set; avoid applying with sulfate or phosphate fertilizers
Secondary / Alternate Option:
Gypsum (if Ca + S needed)
or Calcium Chloride foliar
Caution / Side Effect: Ca is absorbed by young tissues only via transpiration; keep soil moisture adequate

Possible Symptom in Plant: Uniform yellowing of new leaves; stunted growth; delayed maturity
Recommended Dose (kg/ha or as noted): 20–40 kg S/ha
Application Method & Timing: Basal incorporation; allow 3–4 weeks for oxidation; wet/warm soil accelerates release
Caution / Side Effect: Sulfur also lowers pH; beneficial in alkaline Telangana soils; monitor pH after application
